results.title: UKG Showcases AI-Led Workforce Orchestration at UNLEASH and TRANSFORM
results.author: PR Newswire
results.text:
UKG Showcases AI-Led Workforce Orchestration at UNLEASH and TRANSFORM

### Sorting by

×

Skip to content

# UKG Showcases AI-Led Workforce Orchestration at UNLEASH and TRANSFORM

PR Newswire|March 19, 2026| Artificial Intelligence, Employee Engagement, HR Technology, Hiring, Human Capital Management

🕧 7 min

#### Real-time workforce platform embeds intelligence into operations, enabling enterprises to anticipate risk, guide decisions, and empower employees in frontline-heavy environments

Through featured speaking sessions and live demonstrations at UNLEASH America 2026 and TRANSFORM 2026, UKG, a leading global AI platform that unifies HR, pay, and workforce management, will showcase its Workforce Operating Platform — a system of action that uses governed, explainable people-first AI to continuously transform real-time workforce signals into outcome-focused intelligence — which provides leaders and managers with transparent, auditable decision support as work unfolds.

Also Read: The Rise of the AI-Augmented Workforce: Redefining Roles, Skills, and Accountability

Powered by agentic and assistive AI, the UKG Workforce Operating Platform is capable of transforming billions of workforce signals into real-time, explainable intelligence and guided, auditable action.

The Real-time Workforce Platform Difference

More than 80,000 organizations worldwide rely on UKG to power mission-critical workforce decisions in complex, frontline-heavy environments. Powered by agentic and assistive AI, the UKG Workforce Operating Platform is capable of transforming billions of workforce signals into real-time, explainable intelligence and guided, auditable action — from overtime optimization and attrition risk detection to payroll integrity, compliance oversight, and high-volume frontline hiring to keep operations staffed. Purpose-built for scale, industry-specific needs, and frontline complexity, the platform embeds AI and intelligence directly into the operational core of work.

Built on UKG’s platform, the UKG Pro® HCM suite is recognized as a Leader in both the 2025 Gartner Magic Quadrant™ for Cloud HCM Suites and the Forrester Wave™ Q4 2025 for HCM. Designed for enterprise organizations with complex HR, payroll, and regulatory requirements, UKG Pro extends beyond traditional HCM through differentiated tools that connect skills, communication, knowledge, compliance, and sentiment into a single intelligence layer, including:

- UKG Talent Marketplace, which uses AI-driven skills intelligence to expand career mobility for people and address critical labor shortages for the business;
- UKG Talk®, which strengthens frontline communication and collaboration across shifts, departments, and locations;
- UKG People Assist™, delivering personalized, on-demand HR support with intelligent case routing;
- UKG Document Manager, providing enterprise-wide visibility and compliance

results.title: UKG Unveils Bryte AI Agents and Capabilities to Transform Employee Experiences for the UKG Pro HCM Suite
results.text:
UKG Unveils Bryte AI Agents and Capabilities to Transform Employee Experiences for the UKG Pro HCM Suite | UKG

UKG, a leading provider of HR, payroll, workforce management, and culture solutions, is launching UKG Bryte™ AI Agents, which are integrated into the UKG Pro® HCM suite, to help customers harness the power of artificial intelligence to create exceptional workplace experiences and streamline people-centric processes.

The UKG Bryte AI Agents complete complicated, multi-step processes and proactively solve problems by utilizing modern AI including Large Action Models (LAM) that combine the language fluency of Large Language Models (LLM) with the capacity to complete tasks and decision-making autonomously to help customers achieve their goals. They continuously improve their performance over time by learning from their experiences, often employing reinforcement learning or supervised learning techniques.

The first wave of UKG Bryte AI Agents will focus on enhancing compliance and talent management, helping customers improve their employee experience. For example, the employee promotion process is often fragmented and highly manual, requiring the execution of multiple, inefficient disconnected tasks. Leveraging the UKG Bryte AI Promotion Agent, UKG is transforming the employee promotion process by streamlining and automating tasks, reducing manual processing time, and mitigating errors in promotion-related workflows.

UKG is also introducing the UKG Bryte AI Continuous Compliance Agent, which allows payroll administrators to quickly respond to ever-changing tax compliance requirements without missing a beat. Instead of spending hours manually tracking, researching, and applying new tax rules, Payroll Admins can utilize the Continuous Compliance agent to identify the impact of new regulations and accelerate the time-consuming setup changes.

“With the rapid evolution of artificial intelligence technology, we are extending our UKG Bryte Generative AI capabilities by developing AI agents that can understand and respond to customer needs in real-time, handling a broad array of tasks, from data analysis, to workflow automation, and customer support,” said Hugo Sarrazin, president, chief product and technology officer at UKG. “Our Bryte AI Agents will work alongside employees and managers, particularly those in frontline roles, taking on tasks that are best suited for technology. This allows people to focus on the aspects of their jobs that have the biggest impact on their organizations.”

With a commitment to responsible and ethical AI utilization, UKG’s connected agents will allow the organization to determine which tasks can be completed autonomously while ensuring there are built-in checkpoints that require people to review and accept. UKG Bryte is trained on the world’s largest collection of people data, work data, and culture data, including three decades of proprietary benchmarks and best practices from

results.title: UKG’s AI-First Future
results.author: Dylan Teggart
results.text:
UKG’s AI-First Future - 3Sixty Insights, Inc.
Skip to content
# UKG’s AI-First Future
Posted onJune 17, 2025July 1, 2025byDylan Teggart
UKG’s AI-First Future - Banner
At its 2025 Analyst Day, UKG doubled down on its commitment to becoming an AI-first company. Unveiling a sweeping strategy designed to transform the workforce experience for both employees and employers.
UKG described how the company is evolving from traditional workforce software to an AI-driven platform grounded in real-time data, extensibility, and industry-specific insight. This transformation is powered by the FleX Platform and People Fabric, which together provide a unified data architecture that fuels AI-powered workflows, intelligent recommendations, and automated task execution.
A centerpiece of UKG’s AI vision is Bryte, its proprietary AI engine. Bryte is embedded throughout the platform to support a wide range of use cases:
* **Bryte Job Genius**generates job descriptions and interview guides.
* **Paycheck AI Agent**answers employee payroll questions and flags discrepancies.
* **Employee Self-Service Agent**assists with scheduling, PTO requests, and pay summaries.
* The**Benefits Formula Editor**utilizes AI to configure complex benefit plans prior to launch.
From frontline enablement to executive-level decision-making, UKG is integrating AI into every corner of its platform, making its tools smarter, more predictive, and personalized. With the HR industry moving beyond automating routine tasks, the result is a scalable, secure, and flexible ecosystem where “AI agents” act as digital collaborators. These agents offload repetitive tasks, such as payroll processing, benefits administration, and scheduling, so employees can focus on higher-value work. UKG’s AI agents and Bryte engine are prime examples of this evolution.
Already, more than 2,500 AI models are active across UKG’s suite, with over 10 million employees interacting with them on a monthly basis. Predictive AI supports workforce planning by identifying trends, such as flight risk and shift fatigue. Generative AI is used to create personalized coaching insights, cultural feedback summaries, and even real-time document Q&A features. For instance, managers can now use conversational reporting tools to explore workforce data and generate dynamic visualizations instantly.
As part of the move to “democratize AI” that many AI-enabled companies have taken, users now expect their workforce tools to behave more like consumer apps—intuitive, personalized, and voice- or chat-enabled. Additionally, recognizing that smaller businesses often lack dedicated IT or HR support, UKG has made AI accessible through guided setup tools. Simplified integrations, no-code tools, and guided AI setup are critical steps in ensuring SMBs aren’t left behind.
Talent development is also seeing real advances, with personalized learning paths that offer candidates, especially high-potential

results.title: UKG Debuts AI-guided User Experience | UKG
results.text:
UKG Debuts AI-guided User Experience | UKG

UKG, a leading global AI platform unifying HR, pay, and workforce management, has unveiled a new AI-guided user experience for the UKG Pro® suite that is now available for customers to turn on. The experience brings consumer-level usability to enterprise software through a completely new, streamlined interface that blends personalization, conversational search, and simplicity. The result is a unified, consistent experience across the entire UKG Pro suite, spanning both HCM and Workforce Management. With the new design, everyday actions like requesting time off, approving timesheets, viewing pay, or updating personal information can now be completed in just a few clicks, emphasizing speed, clarity, and user delight.

UKG Bryte™ AI introduces a conversational interface that reimagines how people interact with workforce technology, moving beyond traditional point-and-click systems to a search- and context-driven experience that’s deeply personalized, more intuitive, and adaptive,” said Suresh Vittal, Chief Product Officer at UKG. “As the world’s Workforce Operating Platform trusted by more than 70% of the Fortune 500 to solve their most complex frontline workforce challenges, we’re proud to lead the way in delivering AI-guided technology designed for people.”

More than 180,000 users around the world participated in new experience beta testing, making it the largest and most successful UKG beta program to date, with nearly three-fourths of all beta testers adopting the new experience. Organizations can already activate the user interface at their own pace, giving them the flexibility to explore the new design and functionality before it becomes the default experience later in 2026. This opt-in approach ensures a smooth transition while letting customers and even their employees gradually transition.

The new experience will be one of many Innovations showcased next week at UKG® Aspire 2025, where approximately 5,000 customers, partners, industry analysts, and influencers will get an interactive look at the next wave of innovations for the UKG’s Workforce Operating Platform. UKG Aspire will take place next week, from Nov. 3-6, at the Mandalay Bay Resort in Las Vegas.

### Supporting Resources

- Learn more about UKG and our leading global AI platform for HR, pay, and workforce management.
- It’s not too late to register for UKG Aspire, taking place Nov. 3-6 at the Mandalay Bay Resort in Las Vegas.
- Follow UKG on Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok, and YouTube.

### About UKG

UKG is a leading global AI platform for HR, pay, and workforce management. Unifying award-winning solutions with the world’s largest collection of workforce data and people-first AI, UKG delivers unrivaled insights into today’s workforce, helping organizations in every industry turn data into decisions
